course_title: Advanced Chinese to English Translation in the Age of AI
course_description:
This course builds on the translation concepts introduced in CHIN2700 and extends their application to texts of both the same and new, more complex, genre. In so doing, an advanced understanding of genre-specific issues in translation from Chinese into English is provided as well as advanced development of context-appropriate English expression.

This course explores advanced Chinese-to-English translation techniques, with a focus on integrating traditional skills and AI-assisted tools to address contemporary challenges. It is divided into modules that train students to translate a range of genres, including political texts, traditional Chinese medicine, Australian history, fiction, climate science, and government health brochures. Students will engage with genre-specific translation strategies, hone their ability to produce linguistically accurate and culturally appropriate translations, and receive guidance on NAATI expectations. Emphasis will be placed on the strategic, responsible use of AI technologies to improve translation accuracy and efficiency. The course Instruction is practical and workshop-based, involving a lot of in-class discussions and interactions. The weekly sessions are delivered live and are not recorded.

restrictions: Restricted to students who are native speakers of Chinese

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
You attend one 2 hour Workshop per week (there is no separate lecture or tutorial).
Public holidays:
Alternative arrangements for affected students will be announced through the Blackboard (Ultra) site.
Class allocation:
In order to optimise the student experience, it may be necessary to reallocate students to a different class from their first choice. Before this happens, every effort will be made to enable students to voluntarily change into an alternative class that is suitable.
Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation. Therefore, should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please ensure you email
hass.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
from your UQ student email account with the following details:
Full name,
Student ID, and
the Course Code
Additional information and support can be found
here
.
course_aims: ﾠThis course aims to trainﾠnative (and near-native) speakers of Chinese in the key issues concerning translation of more advanced genres from Chinese into English

learning_outcomes.description: translate more advanced text genres from Chinese into English in a linguistically and culturally appropriate mannner
appreciate linguistic and cultural differences across more complex text genres
be aware of the key cultural and ethical issues in translation, as set out by NAATI
access and understand the application of translation resources
have a well-rounded linguistic vocabulary to talk about texts

assessment_details.title: Weekly e-quizzes on Bb (Ultra)
assessment_details.due_date: WEEK 2 - 6/03/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 3 - 13/03/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 4 - 20/03/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 5 - 27/03/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 6 - 2/04/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 7 - 17/04/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 8 - 24/04/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 9 - 1/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 10 - 8/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 11 - 15/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 12 - 22/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 13 - 29/05/2026 5:00 pm Due every Friday, WEEKS 2-13 at 5pm except in WEEK 6, when it is due at 5pm on Thursday as Friday of that week is a Public Holiday.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Type: E-quizzes on Bb (Ultra) Reading: 0 minutes Duration: 20 minutes Format: Translation and multiple-choice Starting from Friday in Week 1, an online quiz will be posted on the "Assessment" page on Ultra each Friday, which you must complete before Friday 5 pm of the following week. For example, Week 2 e-quiz will be open on Friday in Week 1 and must be submitted on Friday of Week 2. Only Week 6 e-quiz is rescheduled to Thursday 5pm due to a public holiday. During even-numbered weeks (i.e., Weeks 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, & 12), each e-quiz comprises 8 multiple-choice questions designed to assess the topics covered in both the current week's and the preceding week's classes. Each of the 8 questions in an e-quiz has only one correct answer. During odd-numbered weeks (i.e., Weeks 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, & 13), the online e-quiz entails a practical translation or a translation revision task comprising 6-8 sentences. This task is based on the material covered in the preceding week(s) and/or the current week. For example, the quiz in Week 3 covers content from Weeks 1, 2, and/or 3, while the quiz in Week 5 focuses on learning materials from Weeks 4 and/or 5, and so forth. You must complete an e-quiz in 20 minutes without interruption . So, make sure to use reliable internet, e.g., UQL. The best 10 e-quiz results for each student will be used to calculate the final mark for this assessment item. STATEMENT ON AI & MT This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via Ultra.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
No extensions are allowed for this series of tasks as students are given a seven-day period to complete them. Individual results and correct answers are automatically released on a weekly basis. Timely engagement with these tasks supports sequential nature of learning in this course. Additionally, only the best 10 e-quiz results out of 12 will be used to calculate the final mark for this assessment item.
Late submission
Late submissions not accepted, as the task remains open for one week.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: Late submissions not accepted, as the task remains open for one week.

assessment_details.title: ONLINE Blackboard (Ultra) In-Semester Exam
assessment_details.due_date: 2/04/2026 6:10 pm Thursday, WEEK 6 - The exam starts at 5pm and is due at 6:10pm.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The in-semester assessment will test students' knowledge and understanding of all the W1-W5 course content, particularly the in-class materials. Students will translate seen texts or texts similar to the in-class materials (25% of exam content) and an unseen text (75% of exam content). The unseen text is approximately 300-320 characters in length. The texts will represent the different genres studied in W1-W5. Students may have access to lecture notes and paper dictionaries during the exam. Exam will be administered online via Blackboard (Ultra). Be sure to access a reliable internet connection. Once you start the exam you must complete it within 70 minutes in one go (60 minutes plus 10 minutes planning time) . The exam is automatically submitted. You may use any online tools to complete this assessment, but all other forms of assistance are prohibited. STATEMENT ON AI & MT This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time 10 minutes Duration 60 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Open book examination - any written or printed material is permitted; material may be annotated Exam platform Other Invigilation Not invigilated
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The exam is automatically submitted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .

assessment_details.title: Oral interview (sight translation)
assessment_details.due_date: 10/06/2026 9:30 am The interviews will start at 9:30am on Wednesday, 10th of June (EXAM WEEK 1). Each student will be assigned a time slot for their interview. If students have time clashes with other exams, please reach out to your class teacher for a rearrangement.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The final oral interview is an in-person sight translation task. It will test students' knowledge and understanding and application of the course content, particularly the in-class materials. Students will be required to sight translate one Chinese text into spoken English. Each document is around 320-350 characters long and will be handed out at the assessment venue. Students will have 10 minutes to prepare for sight translating each document and need to finish the task within the time limit of 7 minutes. AI and MT STATEMENT This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
Each student must complete the task within 7 minutes. The teacher or examiner may terminate the exam at the 8-minute mark.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: Each student must complete the task within 7 minutes. The teacher or examiner may terminate the exam at the 8-minute mark.
